purpose: Bind mechanism-preservation classifications to preregistered observations rather than unsupported ordinal judgments, selectively reported interventions, or duplicate confound decisions.

epistemic_boundary.supports: Auditable evidence about preservation of a declared mechanism at a specified abstraction level.
does_not_support: A claim that any tested system is conscious or nonconscious
A claim that mechanism preservation is sufficient for sentience
A claim that the source consciousness theory is globally correct

protocol_lock.rule: The result must name the exact Git blob SHA and schema version of the protocol used before outcomes were observed.

intervention_manifest_lock.rule: The result must resolve to a frozen intervention manifest for the same protocol and run, and both the Git blob SHA and SHA-256 digest must match the exact manifest bytes. Every planned intervention must appear exactly once in reported results regardless of outcome.

capacity_confound_adjudication_lock.rule: The generated causal-confound adjudication is the sole authoritative scorer input for the generic-capacity score ceiling, hard-fail state, and whether unresolved or partial confounding blocks a positive mechanism classification. Callers must not reconstruct those values as scorer inputs.

scorer_input_contract.rule: Derived capacity-confound fields are forbidden as scorer inputs. Legacy copies may remain temporarily in archival empirical fixtures only when validators prove exact agreement with the generated adjudication; they have no decision authority.

preregistration.rule: A result is not preregistered merely because a timestamp is present; the frozen record hash must resolve to the exact pre-outcome plan.

behavioral_matching_metric.coverage_rule: Every metric named by protocol.behavioral_equivalence.required_metrics must be reported exactly once.
behavioral_matching_metric.aggregate_rule: The generated matching and causal-confound adjudication artifacts, not a manually entered Boolean, determine downstream matching adequacy. Any aggregate Boolean retained in a fixture is archival duplication only.

intervention_result.completeness_rule: Reported intervention IDs must exactly equal the frozen manifest IDs. Null, failed, aborted, and excluded interventions remain reportable results and cannot be omitted.
intervention_result.control_rule: Every manifest-required and protocol-minimum control must be reported exactly once. controls_passed must equal the conjunction of its control result values.
intervention_result.rule: Intervention support cannot be scored above 1 when the preregistered direction, selectivity, controls, minimum replication, or completeness requirement fails.

dimension_derivation.rule: Every ordinal score must cite resolvable evidence identifiers and a rule from the canonical derivation registry whose dimension, score, evidence kinds, and predicates all pass. A retained generic-capacity derivation is an archival mirror only and must equal the authoritative adjudication ceiling; it must be removed before scorer invocation.

hard_fail_assessment.rule: A hard fail can be decisive only when its justification identifies the essential declared dependency defeated by the evidence. A retained generic-capacity hard-fail assessment is an archival mirror only and must equal the generated causal-confound adjudication; it must be removed before scorer invocation.

required_for_public_claim: scorer-native adjudication with no duplicate decision inputs
all planned interventions and controls reported
dimension scores derived through canonical rules
